blanks.sentence: Worldwide spending on snacks reaches approximately _____ annually
blanks.evidence: "snacks are big business, with an estimated $1.5 trillion a year spent globally on snacking."

blanks.sentence: New products like the _____ have emerged
blanks.evidence: "From old favourites like popcorn and crisps to new inventions like the chocolate sandwich, snacks are big business"
"I like salted peanuts and I love a banana – preferably together, actually. I find that they hit the spot."
"My favourite snack is called 'chipa'. It's a small, round bun made with cassava, cheese, butter, milk and salt."
"It's a small, round bun made with cassava, cheese, butter, milk and salt."

blanks.sentence: Snacks serve as an _____ for consumers
blanks.evidence: "A snack is an affordable luxury, so if you've had a bad day or money is tight, this is something that you can turn to"

blanks.sentence: Remain popular even when _____ for many people
blanks.evidence: "if you've had a bad day or money is tight, this is something that you can turn to"
"Consumers, especially in the United States, are looking for smaller, more convenient portions."
"They're not always going to be able to sit down and have three meals a day, right?"
"So, they're eating on the go."
"this desire for very specific functional ingredients, right? So, mental clarity, gut health, muscle development"

blanks.sentence: Target improvements in _____, gut health and muscle development
blanks.evidence: "So, mental clarity, gut health, muscle development, these sorts of things."
"Again, they're nicely packaged in snack sizes."
"Personally, I enjoy cooking and eating more natural, simpler snacks"

blanks.evidence: "b) confectionery and chocolate, which 45% of Britons indulge in weekly"

blanks.sentence: Approximately _____ of British people consume these weekly
blanks.evidence: "b) confectionery and chocolate, which 45% of Britons indulge in weekly, according to that survey."

multipleChoice.question: What is Annie Gray's attitude towards her preferred snack combination?
options: She is uncertain whether it is a healthy choice
She is genuinely satisfied with how well it meets her needs
She is embarrassed to admit her unusual preference
She is concerned about the nutritional value
multipleChoice.evidence: Annie Gray: "I like salted peanuts and I love a banana – preferably together, actually. I find that they hit the spot."

multipleChoice.question: Why does Neil admit to loving crisps despite wanting to mention something healthier?
options: He believes crisps are actually nutritious
He is trying to impress Beth with his honesty
He feels pressured by the survey results
He is being candid about his genuine preference
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil: "Well, I want to say something healthy, but actually, you know, I really love a bag of crisps."

multipleChoice.question: According to Christine Cochran, what is the main reason snacks remain popular even during difficult economic times?
options: They provide an inexpensive form of indulgence
They are heavily advertised to consumers
They contain essential nutritional ingredients
They are easier to prepare than regular meals
multipleChoice.evidence: Christine Cochran: "A snack is an affordable luxury, so if you've had a bad day or money is tight, this is something that you can turn to"

multipleChoice.question: What change in eating habits does Christine Cochran observe among American consumers?
options: They are increasingly adopting vegetarian diets
They are returning to traditional home cooking
They are shifting away from conventional meal schedules
They are consuming larger portions at each sitting
multipleChoice.evidence: Christine Cochran: "They're not always going to be able to sit down and have three meals a day, right? So, they're eating on the go."

multipleChoice.question: What is Christine Cochran's view on functional ingredients in snacks?
options: They cater to consumers' specific health objectives
They represent unnecessary marketing gimmicks
They should replace traditional snack ingredients
They are too expensive for average consumers
multipleChoice.evidence: Christine Cochran: "this desire for very specific functional ingredients… So, mental clarity, gut health, muscle development"

multipleChoice.question: What does the term "mental clarity" describe in the context of snack benefits?
options: A focused and engaged state of mind
The ability to read ingredient labels carefully
A marketing strategy targeting educated buyers
The transparent packaging of modern snacks
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil: "mental clarity – a term describing a state of mind which is focused and engaged, not dull or foggy."

multipleChoice.question: Why does Barbara Oievari say she "can't swim against the current"?
options: She lacks confidence in her cooking abilities
Her son refuses to eat anything homemade
She finds it impractical to resist prevailing trends
She has limited access to natural ingredients
multipleChoice.evidence: Barbara: "but of course I can't swim against the current, and if my 9-year-old wants to eat the little baked snacks that have cheddar…"

multipleChoice.question: What similarity does Barbara Oievari notice between her son's snack and her own preference?
options: They both originate from Argentinian cuisine
They both feature cheese as a key component
They both are considered unhealthy options
They both require baking in an oven
multipleChoice.evidence: Barbara: "ironically it is something baked with cheese in it, which is similar to what I enjoy"

multipleChoice.question: What function does Beth's comment "all those snacks have made me hungry" serve in the conversation?
options: To genuinely express her physical hunger
To introduce the revelation of the quiz answer
To criticize the discussion of unhealthy foods
To propose ending the recording session
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth: "Well, Neil, all those snacks have made me hungry! Now, isn't it time to reveal the answer to your question?"

multipleChoice.question: According to the YouGov survey mentioned in the programme, what proportion of British people consume confectionery and chocolate weekly?
options: Around 45% partake in them regularly
More than half select them as favorites
Approximately one-third prefer them
Nearly 60% consume them daily
multipleChoice.evidence: Neil: "And the answer was… b) confectionery and chocolate, which 45% of Britons indulge in weekly, according to that survey."
